data.title: Encora Therapeutics receives FDA clearance for Encora X1™ wearable device for Essential Tremor, after successful clinical studies.
data.description: In Boston, Encora Therapeutics, a prominent non-invasive neuromodulation technology company, revealed that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved the Encora X1™ after granting 510(k) clearance. This decision was based on information derived from two key clinical studies: a randomized, sham-controlled trial and a 90-day home-use study. The studies illustrated significant enhancements in tremor severity and performance of practical tasks, highlighting the device's efficacy in assisting with daily activities.

data.title: Optimum Communications Q4 earnings: EPS -0.15 (missed estimate by -1400%), revenue down $53M from last year.
data.description: On Thursday, February 12, 2026, Optimum Communications (NYSE: OPTU) released its Q4 earnings report at 07:00 AM. Additionally, revenue decreased by $53.00 million from the previous year. data.title: Iridium reports 2025 financial results and issues 2026 outlook; a Nasdaq company.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, in McLean, Virginia, Iridium Communications Inc. (Nasdaq: IRDM) announced its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company, renowned for offering worldwide voice, data, and PNT satellite services, has shared details indicating its progress. Additionally, Iridium has provided guidance for its expectations for the entire year of 2026. This report marks a significant milestone for Iridium Communications Inc., demonstrating their ongoing commitment to transparency and accountability in the market.

data.title: Palantir's PFCS Forward gets DISA authorization, extending IL5 and IL6 to on-premises and edge deployments.
data.description: In Denver, Palantir Technologies Inc. disclosed that the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) has approved Palantir Federal Cloud Service (PFCS) Forward, broadening PFCS's current Impact Level 5 and Impact Level 6 Provisional Authorizations to cover on-premises and edge installations. This authorization enables deployment of Palantir's tech stack, comprising Apollo, Gotham, Foundry, and AIP platforms, along with infrastructure like Rubix. The move expands the reach of PFCS, with NASDAQ-listed PLTR making the announcement.

data.title: Akins Ford in Winder, GA, welcomes 2026 Ford F-450 Super Duty trucks for fleet owners and truck enthusiasts.
data.description: A significant stock of the 2026 Ford F-450 has been delivered to Akins Ford in Winder, Georgia. This dealership, located near Atlanta, is now offering this popular truck to cater to the needs of local fleet owners and truck enthusiasts. The arrival of this inventory comes as good news on February 12, 2026. This massive selection at Akins Ford provides a wide range of choices for customers, reflecting the high demand for the Ford F-450 model. With this new stock, individuals can explore various options of this sought-after vehicle to suit their preferences.

data.title: Adept Materials introduces Lilypad, a paint that manages indoor humidity, addressing multi-billion-dollar moisture control.
data.description: A new product called Lilypad™, developed by Adept Materials, has been introduced to address the issue of moisture control indoors. This innovative paint and primer system, launched in SOMERVILLE, Mass., on Feb. 12, 2026, is designed to naturally manage indoor humidity levels. Lilypad™ aims to tackle the multi-billion-dollar problem associated with moisture control in indoor environments.

data.title: Crocs, Inc. outperforms in 2025 with revenue & earnings; repurchases 6.5M shares for $577M. Expects EPS growth in 2026.
data.description: 2025 proved to be a successful year for Crocs, Inc. as their full-year results exceeded expectations with strong figures in revenue and earnings per share. The company showcased robust cash flow, which allowed them to repurchase 6.5 million shares amounting to $577 million in the same year. Looking ahead to 2026, Crocs, Inc. is optimistic about delivering growth in earnings per share. The company is based in Broomfield, Colorado and made this announcement on February 12, 2026.

data.title: Noel Geoffroy named Global CEO of Vetnique Labs, pet health & wellness company. Announcement made on Feb. 12, 2026.
data.description: A prominent consumer industry leader has been appointed by Vetnique Labs, a top pet health and wellness company, to spearhead its future growth. Based in Naperville, Illinois, the company made the announcement on February 12, 2026. Noel Geoffroy will be taking the helm to lead the company into its next phase of expansion. Vetnique Labs is known for offering vet-recommended products in the pet health sector, and this move signifies a strategic step towards further development.

data.title: Green Street releases 2026 sector outlooks for 10 sectors in Europe and North America, including global data center market.
data.description: Green Street, a top provider of global commercial real estate (CRE) and infrastructure intelligence, has unveiled its 2026 Annual Sector Outlooks covering 10 sectors in Europe and North America, along with the global data center market. The reports were released on February 12, 2026, in Newport Beach, California. This comprehensive analysis delves into key trends and projections impacting various industries, offering valuable insights for investors and stakeholders.

data.title: The Biological Computing Co. (TBC) raises $25M seed funding, pioneering neuron-based AI alternative to silicon.
data.description: A significant amount of funding has been received by The Biological Computing Co. (TBC), facilitating the rapid deployment of biological computing systems. The co-founders have inaugurated their main laboratory in San Francisco. This announcement was made on February 12, 2026. TBC is at the forefront of revolutionizing computing to cater to the needs of the post-silicon AI age by introducing a novel category of computing technology.

data.title: Diebold Nixdorf doubled free cash flow in FY 2025, with revenue growth of 2% YoY, and established a new 2026 financial outlook exceeding previous targets from 2025. Order entry grew by 17%.
data.description: The company has set a new financial forecast for 2026, surpassing the earlier projections from the 2025 Investor Day. Order entry increased by 17%, while revenue saw a year-over-year growth of around 2%. Free cash flow, on the other hand, more than doubled compared to the previous year, reaching a record high for the company. Adjusted earnings per share also doubled year-over-year. As part of their plans, the company is currently implementing a new $200 million share buyback program.

data.title: Materion beat Q4 estimated earnings by 3.33% with EPS of $1.55, revenue up $52.88M YoY. Last quarter EPS miss led to 0.53% share price drop.
data.description: Materion, listed on the NYSE under the symbol MTRN, disclosed its Q4 financial results on February 12, 2026, at 06:47 AM. The company exceeded expected earnings by 3.33%, with an EPS of $1.55 compared to the estimated $1.5. Revenue saw a $52.88 million increase from the previous year. In the previous quarter, Materion fell short on EPS by $0.0, leading to a 0.53% decrease in share price. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
